Can stagnation coexist with prepare or does Stagnation override Prepare? 

For example, if enemy has stagnated a Lily's strike to protect with prepare1, will it initally cost 5, then go to 4, then 3... , etc , or will it remain at cost 5?
A card can have both
will the card initially cost 5 then decrease by 1 each passing turn, or stagnation overrides prepare?
prepare will still take effect even with stagnation

Well, do still read Mouchette's kit <:karen_Feelguilty:1468258923009282140> 
She procs a follow-up attack whenever you use two strikes, regardless of who they belong to
All her cards, including the follow-up attack, count as strikes
Her exalt boosts strike damage, her rouse gives her a big damage buff after each follow-up attack
Helot's exalt is a big multiplier to all strike damage, and the devour effect helps you with filling your hand with strikes

how to play gmurphy as carry
3 cost to build tent dmg
then 3 cost to do dmg

Waltz in blue stance while preparing, use white stance to tank -> Rouse -> Switch to red stance -> Exalt -> Use all your waltzes -> Hope the enemy dies, or you are cursed to stall for 2 more turns before you can deal damage again

Story is easy until ch5. At ch4 it already becomes a little harder though. You will get more standard units in no time but judging based on what you got now I'd say Goliath + Caecus is your best bet. Then you improvise and add either Caro (free heals and aliemus) or Chaos. Faint is a great sustain and Aigis is a reliable vuln applier. For chaos you can just go any starter characters like Ramona or Ogier and call it a day. Sanga and Celeste are pretty good too

Anyone know a strategy to beat 9-12 normal?

That's ch9 final stage right?
Bring gluttony wheel and use blood pool to increase your max hp.
First 7 turns of the boss has infinite hp bars so damaging it just delays an attack and reset your buffs.
Don't get debuff by boss, you can avoid debuff by delaying their attacks
The rest just depends on how good your team is at using chapter gimmick

Does someone know how Murphy works? I got her from the beginner selection banner because i thought she looked cool but I can't understand her abilities

strike def = self explanatory
the 0 cost just generates a shield and inflicts delayed sacrifice on u, it converts to sacrifice next turn
the 3 cost removes half your sacrifice (and delayed sacrifice)
the exalt gives shield and makes enemies do half dmg but 50% of the dmg taken will be added as sacrifice
 sacrifice is just how much dmg u take at the end of ur turn, it halves itself 
so if u have 10 sacrifice, u take 10 dmg then it becomes 5 sacrifice left
